header.headline: Hungarian Certificates: What You Need and How to Get It
header.description: Life certificates, good conduct certificates, civil status certificates, and proof of citizenship — this guide explains each one and the consul's role.

freeCallout.label: Free Service
freeCallout.text: Life certificates (életbizonyítvány) are provided at no charge to Hungarian pension recipients.

certificates.name: Life Certificate
certificates.body: Required annually by Hungarian pension authorities (NYUFIG) to confirm that the recipient is still living. Without it, pension payments may be suspended. The Honorary Consul signs the certificate in your presence — it is then submitted directly to Hungarian pension authorities.
whatToBring: Hungarian pension documentation showing your recipient number (nyugdíjtörzs-számodra)
certificates.processingNote: Completed same-day at your appointment. Submit directly to NYUFIG or your pension office.

certificates.name: Certificate of Good Conduct
certificates.body: A criminal background certification issued by Hungarian authorities. Required for certain employment, immigration, and citizenship applications. The Honorary Consul authenticates supporting documents for the application — the certificate itself is issued by the Hungarian Criminal Records Office (Igazságügyi Minisztérium).
whatToBring: Application form from the Hungarian Ministry of Justice (if requesting authentication of supporting documents)
Cash or check for authentication fees
certificates.processingNote: The certificate is issued by Hungary, not the consulate. The consul authenticates supporting documents only.

certificates.name: Civil Status Certificate
certificates.body: Certified extracts from Hungarian civil registry records — birth, marriage, or death. Issued by Hungarian municipal offices. The Honorary Consul can authenticate copies of these documents for use in the US or for re-submission to Hungarian authorities.
whatToBring: Cash or check for authentication fees
certificates.processingNote: Original certificates are issued by Hungarian municipal offices. The consul certifies copies.

certificates.name: Proof of Citizenship
certificates.body: Confirmation of Hungarian citizenship status. May be required for employment, travel, or legal proceedings in the US. The Honorary Consul can authenticate Hungarian citizenship documents and provide supporting letters where appropriate.
whatToBring: Hungarian passport or certificate of citizenship
Cash or check for authentication fees
certificates.processingNote: Citizenship is determined by Hungary. The consul authenticates existing citizenship documentation.

cta.heading: Need a Certificate?
cta.body: Book an appointment for life certificates, authentication of supporting documents, or guidance on the certificate process.
